title: Cookie Dough Bliss Balls
summary: Cookie dough fans, this one’s for you. These nutty, fudgy bliss balls taste like the real deal but are made with all the good stuff like dates, almond flour & cashew butter. Ideal for when you want a sweet hit without turning the oven on.

method.description: Roughly chop the dark chocolate. Chuck most of it into a food processor with the cashew butter, oat flour, almond flour, dates, vanilla extract & protein powder (if using). Blitz until a sticky dough forms.
Scoop out the mixture and roll into roughly 12 small balls. Press the remaining chopped chocolate into the outsides of the balls.
Pop them into the fridge to chill for at least 1 hr until firm.
Once set, transfer them to a Tupperware lined with baking paper – add another sheet between layers if you’re stacking them. Keep in the fridge for up to a week or freeze for up to 3 months.

tips: Store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 1 week.
These freeze beautifully – just defrost at room temp for 30 mins before eating.
Perfect for batch prepping – double up & stash some away for future snack attacks.

chefs.name: Rachel Pamart
chefs.summary: Rachel is the founder of Rawlala, a cold-pressed juicery based in East London. She came to clean, holistic eating by tuning into what made her feel most alive and now she channels that into expert, ingredient-led recipes designed to nourish, energise, and taste damn good.
